reason: Physics slice C adds depth-buttressed bracing: a set file absorbs knockback up to its column's capacity and resists knockdown more. The effect (a deep phalanx moving less after a charge than a lone man) requires a before/after comparison to see and isn't legible standalone. Behaviour is pinned by GUT tests. Prone has no visual yet (#300), so a clip would still look like 'charged troops die differently' with no clear signal.
